# ADR 0006: Drop PHP 7.x Support, Require PHP 8.0+

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted — v4.0.0 (2022-01-21)

## Context

PHP 7.2 and 7.3 reached end-of-life in November 2020 and December 2021 respectively. Maintaining cross-version compatibility constrains type system usage (union types, named arguments, `match` expressions, constructor property promotion) and complicates CI matrix size. PHP 8.0 introduced significant improvements used by modern library code.

## Decision

In v4.0.0, PHP 7.2 and 7.3 were dropped and the `composer.json` platform requirement was raised to `^8.0`. PHP 8.4 CI coverage was added in v4.1.3. The range validator was also updated to use `float` instead of `int` (a BC break motivated by API accuracy), bundled in the same major version bump.

Evidence: `CHANGELOG.md` v4.0.0 entry, `composer.json` `"php": "^8.0"`, commit `88440f3`.

## Consequences

- Clean PHP 8+ codebase; no 7.x compatibility shims needed.
- Users on PHP 7.x must stay on v3.x (still maintained for security, but no new features).
- The float-based range validator (also v4.0) is a behavior change alongside the platform drop — both are BC breaks taken together in the major bump.
